title: ceratophyllum platyacanthum subsp. oryzetorum
intro: Ceratophyllum platyacanthum subsp. oryzetorum, commonly known as the rice-leaf coontail, is a subspecies of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Ceratophyllaceae, characterized by its distinctive, feathery leaves resembling rice grains. Traditionally, this plant has been valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and phenolic acids. The health benefits associated with Ceratophyllum platyacanthum subsp. oryzetorum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
